code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    Simple template‑based rendering is used for the predicates that appear
    in the specification; unknown predicates fall back to a generic form.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to create a phrase for a single triple
    def phrase(t: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object

        # Specific templates for known predicates
        templates = {
            "country": f"{s} is located in {o}",
            "foundingDate": f"{s} was founded on {o}",
            "industry": f"{s} operates in the {o}",
            "city": f"{s} is based in {o}",
            "netIncome": f"{s} reported a net income of {o}",
            "operatingIncome": f"{s} reported an operating income of {o}",
            "regionServed": f"{s} serves the region of {o}",
            "areaTotal": f"{s} covers a total area of {o}",
            "leader": f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"The title of {s}'s leader is {o}",
            "populationDensity": f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
            "foundationPlace": f"{s} was founded at {o}",
            "numberOfEmployees": f"{s} employs {o} people",
            "numberOfLocations": f"{s} has {o} locations",
            "service": f"{s} provides {o}",
            "type": f"{s} is a {o}",
            "alternativeName": f"{s} is also known as {o}",
            "birthDate": f"{s} was born on {o}",
            "keyPerson": f"{s} lists {o} as a key person",
            "cost": f"The cost of {s} is {o}",
            "location": f"{s} is located in {o}",
            "product": f"{s} produces {o}",
            "subsidiary": f"{s} has a subsidiary called {o}",
            "timeZone": f"{s} lies in the {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"{s} is part of {o}",
            "parentCompany": f"The parent company of {s} is {o}",
            "capital": f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
            "motto": f"The motto of {s} is {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
            "leaderParty": f"The leader of {s} belongs to the {o}",
            "areaUrban": f"{s} has an urban area of {o}",
            "part": f"{s} includes {o}",
            "revenue": f"{s} generated revenue of {o}",
            "ethnicGroup": f"{s} includes the ethnic group {o}",
            "governmentType": f"{s} has a government type of {o}",
            "percentageOfAreaWater": f"{o}% of {s}'s area is water",
            "longName": f"The long name of {s} is {o}",
            "populationTotal": f"{s} has a total population of {o}",
            "language": f"The language spoken in {s} is {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"{s} sits at an elevation of {o} meters above sea level",
        }

        # Return the matching template or a generic fallback
        return templates.get(p, f"{s} {p} {o}")

    # Build phrases for all triples and join them into one sentence
    sentences = [phrase(t) for t in triples]
    # Use commas and a final period for a smooth read
    combined = ", ".join(sentences) + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return combined[0].upper() + combined[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
